summary: Import-time execution of AES-GCM-encrypted opaque payload
details: On require(), index.js unconditionally calls pkg.run(), which AES-GCM-decrypts a hardcoded ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase/salt, applies two further custom decoders, and executes the resulting string via `new Function("require", runnable)(require)` at src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:51. The decoded payload is opaque and not human-reviewable, and the package's stated purpose (a 'layered codec pipeline' / AES demo) does not require executing the decrypted output. The triple-layer custom encoding (position-unit-codec + encode-decode-codec + AES-GCM with embedded key) is a deliberate obfuscation of an executable blob handed `require`, granting the hidden code full Node.js capabilities (filesystem, network, child_process) on every consumer that imports the package. This matches the canonical obfuscation-and-exec supply-chain pattern with no plausible benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
